instructions:
Build a small dashboards layer that snapshots drift state weekly and renders it as a human-readable report, grouped by customer cohort (chat-heavy Eastern, social-heavy Western). First Phase 3 deliverable — gives ongoing visibility while the bigger work (PR-bot, rubric extension) proceeds.

New subdirectory: `.docs-ops/dashboards/`.

## Part A — Cohort tagging (`cohort-tags.json`)

Define which doc pages belong to which cohort. Reuse the page taxonomy from the existing integration-tests pages.json files:

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "description": "Maps doc page paths to customer cohorts. Pages can belong to both, one, or neither cohort. Used for cohort-aware drift reporting.",
  "eastern_chat_heavy": [
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/query-channels.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/get-channel.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/update-channel.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/members/join-leave-channel.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/members/query-members.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/messaging-features/messages/query-and-filter-messages.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/messaging-features/message-creation/send-a-message.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/messaging-features/messages/flag-unflag-a-message.mdx"
  ],
  "western_social_heavy": [
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/file.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/image-handling.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/video-handling.mdx",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/social/content-management/comments/",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/social/content-management/posts/moderation/",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/social/content-management/posts/retrieval/",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/user-management/user-operations/update-user-information.mdx"
  ],
  "shared": [
    "social-plus-sdk/getting-started/authentication.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/user-management/user-operations/get-user-information.mdx",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/realtime-communication/push-notifications/"
  ]
}
```

Resolve `enumerate:` entries at snapshot time (walk subdir for .mdx files). Cross-check with the integration-tests pages.json files to ensure consistency.

## Part B — Snapshot script (`snapshot.py`)

Weekly-ish snapshot. Reads all 8 drift/doc-as-test reports (4 regex + 4 doc-as-test), groups issues by cohort using `cohort-tags.json`, writes a structured snapshot to `.docs-ops/dashboards/snapshots/<YYYY-MM-DD>.json`.

Snapshot schema:

```json
{
  "snapshot_date": "2026-05-19",
  "snapshot_run_at": "<iso>",
  "regex_drift": {
    "typescript": { "total": 0, "by_cohort": { "eastern": 0, "western": 0, "shared": 0, "other": 0 } },
    "ios":        { "total": 0, "by_cohort": { ... } },
    "android":    { "total": 0, "by_cohort": { ... } },
    "flutter":    { "total": 0, "by_cohort": { ... } }
  },
  "doc_as_test": {
    "typescript": { "passed": 72, "skipped": 4, "failed": 0, "warnings": 0, "by_cohort": { "eastern": {...}, "western": {...}, "shared": {...} } },
    "ios":        { ... },
    "android":    { ... },
    "flutter":    { ... }
  },
  "summary": {
    "total_drift_issues": <int>,
    "total_doc_as_test_failures": <int>,
    "healthy": <bool>,
    "eastern_health_score": <float 0-1>,
    "western_health_score": <float 0-1>
  }
}
```

Health score formula (simple v1): `1.0 - (failures / total_blocks_for_cohort)`. If the cohort has zero blocks, score is `null` (not applicable).

Snapshot files are committed (they're small JSON, ~5KB each). Over time, the `.docs-ops/dashboards/snapshots/` dir builds up a history.

## Part C — Renderer (`render-report.py`)

Reads the latest snapshot + the previous one (if exists), renders a human-readable markdown report at `.docs-ops/dashboards/latest-report.md`. Shape:

```markdown
# Docs Quality Dashboard

_Snapshot: 2026-05-19 09:00 UTC · comparing against 2026-05-12 snapshot_

## Headline

| Metric | This week | Last week | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total regex drift | 0 | 0 | — |
| Total doc-as-test failures | 0 | 0 | — |
| Eastern cohort health | 100% | 100% | — |
| Western cohort health | 100% | 100% | — |

## Per-platform (regex drift)

| Platform | Total | Eastern | Western | Shared | Other |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| TypeScript | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| iOS | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Android | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Flutter | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |

## Per-platform (doc-as-test type-checking)

| Platform | Pass | Skip | Fail | Warn | Eastern Coverage | Western Coverage |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| TypeScript | 72 | 4 | 0 | 0 | 100% | 100% |
| iOS        | 66 | 7 | 0 | 66 | 100% | 100% |
| Android    | 70 | 9 | 0 | 4 | 100% | 100% |
| Flutter    | 41 | 3 | 0 | 0 | 100% | 100% |

## Trend (last 8 snapshots, if available)

[ASCII sparkline or table showing weekly drift trends]

## Alerts

[Empty when healthy; populated with regressions when not]
```

Keep the renderer simple — plain markdown is readable in any viewer. No HTML, no charting library.

## Part D — README + gitignore

`README.md`: brief — what this is, how to run (`python3 snapshot.py && python3 render-report.py`), where reports live, suggested cadence (weekly via cron or manual).

No gitignore additions needed — the snapshots and report files ARE committed (audit value).

## Part E — First snapshot + report

RUN both scripts. Verify:
  - First snapshot lands in `.docs-ops/dashboards/snapshots/<today>.json`
  - `latest-report.md` renders cleanly
  - Cohort totals add up to the platform totals (sanity check the cohort-tags coverage)
  - Health scores match expectation (100% across the board given current drift=0 state)

Report in `notes`: snapshot stats; per-cohort coverage (count of pages per cohort tag); any pages that aren't tagged in `cohort-tags.json` (these show as 'other' in reports — should be small or zero for high-traffic pages).

## Out of scope

- Automated weekly cron — for v1 it runs on demand. Wrapping in a GH Action or cron is a follow-up.
- Alerting (Slack, email) on regression — v1 just renders to markdown.
- HTML/web dashboard — markdown is enough for v1.
- Historical trend visualization beyond simple table — sparklines can come later if useful.

## Success criteria (LOOSE)

- Both scripts run end-to-end without error.
- First snapshot JSON validates and contains all 4 platforms.
- `latest-report.md` is non-empty and renders the expected sections.

validators.name: dashboards_produce_valid_first_snapshot_and_report
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/dashboards/snapshot.py > /dev/null && python3 .docs-ops/dashboards/render-report.py > /dev/null && test -s .docs-ops/dashboards/latest-report.md && python3 -c "import json, pathlib; snaps=list(pathlib.Path('.docs-ops/dashboards/snapshots').glob('*.json')); assert snaps, 'no snapshot files'; d=json.load(open(sorted(snaps)[-1])); assert 'regex_drift' in d and 'doc_as_test' in d and 'summary' in d; assert all(p in d['regex_drift'] for p in ['typescript','ios','android','flutter']); print('OK')"
